svg.text: Hash Table — The O(1) Lookup Workhorse
• Set — membership tests in O(1). "Have I seen this value before?"
• Map — key → metadata. Index, count, last position, frequency.
• Counter — multiset semantics. Anagram tests, frequency comparisons.
O(n) extra space buys O(1) average lookup. Worst-case O(n) per op on collisions —
in adversarial / contest settings, randomise the hash or use a balanced tree.
• "What was the index of the last X?" → map (key → index)
• "Pair (a, b) with a + b = k" → map of complements
• Group by transform (sorted-string, signature) → map of bucket → list
If you find yourself searching a list repeatedly, swap it for a hash structure.
